ocr.text: The recruitment of participants for clinical trials is a critical component of successful research outcomes, particularly in the
context of the Iberoamerican Cochrane Centre and the Institute of Biomedical Research (IIB Sant Pau) in Barcelona. As
demonstrated in the accompanying data table, various recruitment centers play a pivotal role in the timely and efficient
gathering of participants, which directly influences the robustness and generalizability of trial findings. The "Total Participants
Recruited" column highlights the varying levels of engagement across different centers, reflecting not only their operational
capabilities but also the effectiveness of their outreach strategies.
Coordinating centre:
Iberoamerican Cochrane Centre. Institute of Biomedical Research (IIB Sant Pau), Barcelona
Recruiters centers:
1  Complexo Hospitalario Universitario A Coruña
2  Hospital Universitario Central de Asturias
3  Complejo Hospitalario Universitario Insular – Materno Infantil de Canarias
4  Hospital Universitario Cruces, Barakaldo.
5  Hospital Universitario Miguel Servet, Zaragoza
6  Hospital Universitario La Paz, Madrid
7  Hospital Nacional de Parapléjicos de Toledo
8  Hospital Universitario Virgen del Rocío, Sevilla
9  Hospital de Neurorrehabilitación - Instituto Guttmann, Badalona
10  Hospital Universitario Puerta del Mar, Cadiz
Furthermore, the "Recruitment Rate" metric provides insight into the efficiency with which these centers convert potential
volunteers into enrolled participants. This information is essential for understanding the relative performance of each center and
identifying best practices that can be shared across the network. By analyzing these metrics, stakeholders can develop targeted
interventions to enhance recruitment efforts, ultimately improving the quality and impact of clinical research in the region. The
strategic evaluation of recruitment data is vital for fostering collaboration and ensuring that clinical trials are adequately
powered to yield meaningful results.
